steps.action: Pendet dance (Balinese: ᬧᭂᬦ᭄ᬤᭂᬢ᭄, igélan pendet) with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Pendet dance (Balinese: ᬧᭂᬦ᭄ᬤᭂᬢ᭄, igélan pendet) with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: Pendet is typically performed by young girls, ca
steps.text: Pendet is typically performed by young girls, carrying bowls of flower petals, handfuls of which are cast into the air at various times in the dance.

steps.action: Pendet can be thought of as a dance of greeting,
steps.text: Pendet can be thought of as a dance of greeting, to welcome the audience and invite spirits to enjoy a performance.

steps.action: It is one of the oldest Balinese dances, althoug
steps.text: It is one of the oldest Balinese dances, although the current form was codified in the 1950s.

steps.action: Traditional dances can be divided into two types
steps.text: Traditional dances can be divided into two types: the sacred dances called Wali and entertainment dances called Bebalihan.

steps.action: Wali (sacred dances) are usually performed only
steps.text: Wali (sacred dances) are usually performed only during certain ritual ceremonies.

steps.action: Balinese Hindus believed that the sacred dances
steps.text: Balinese Hindus believed that the sacred dances has strong religious significance and spiritual power, thus can only be performed during specific religious ceremony by specific dancers.

steps.action: Bebalihan are usually performed in social events
steps.text: Bebalihan are usually performed in social events.

steps.action: Pendet is considered as a form of Bebalihan, oft
steps.text: Pendet is considered as a form of Bebalihan, often performed secularly to welcome guests or audiences.

steps.action: By the 1950s, the dance choreography, movements,
steps.text: By the 1950s, the dance choreography, movements, costumes and properties were codified by two artists from Sumertha village, Denpasar; I Wayan Rindi and Ni Ketut Reneng.

steps.action: Both of them created Pendet as a welcome dance w
steps.text: Both of them created Pendet as a welcome dance with four dancers, performed as part of a tourism show at a number of hotels in Denpasar, Bali.

steps.action: In 1961, I Wayan Beratha developed this dance an
steps.text: In 1961, I Wayan Beratha developed this dance and increased the number of dancers to five, as is often performed now.

steps.action: In 1962, I Wayan Beratha and his colleagues deve
steps.text: In 1962, I Wayan Beratha and his colleagues developed Pendet dance as a mass dance.

steps.action: The number of dancers was no less than 800 peopl
steps.text: The number of dancers was no less than 800 people, and featured in the opening ceremony of the Asian Games in Jakarta.

steps.action: Pendet is the presentation of an offering in the
steps.text: Pendet is the presentation of an offering in the form of a ritual dance.

steps.action: Unlike sacred ritual dances that demand arduous
steps.text: Unlike sacred ritual dances that demand arduous training, Pendet may be danced by anyone, taught simply by imitation.

steps.action: As a religious dance, Pendet is usually performe
steps.text: As a religious dance, Pendet is usually performed during temple ceremonies.

steps.action: All dancers carry in their right hand a small si
steps.text: All dancers carry in their right hand a small silver bowl containing offerings include colorful flower petals and incense.

steps.action: The dance describes that the maiden dance from s
steps.text: The dance describes that the maiden dance from shrine to shrine within the temple.

steps.action: Pendet may be performed intermittently throughou
steps.text: Pendet may be performed intermittently throughout the day and late into the night during temple feasts.

steps.action: Pendet dancers bring flowers in small Bokor, sil
steps.text: Pendet dancers bring flowers in small Bokor, silver bowls containing flowers in a ceremony.

steps.action: At the end of the performance, the maiden dancer
steps.text: At the end of the performance, the maiden dancers would throw and sprinkle the flower petals towards the guests.

steps.action: This dance is a symbol of welcoming gods, spirit
steps.text: This dance is a symbol of welcoming gods, spirits, and guests in some ritual ceremonies in Bali.

steps.action: Pendet actually has simple dance movements.
steps.text: Pendet actually has simple dance movements.
